div.head: The Second Secretary of Embassy in the Soviet Union (Thompson) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 71. My 68, March 12, 3 p.m.Not printed. GarreauRoger Garreau, delegate of the French National Committee with personal rank of Minister; he had arrived in Moscow on March 8, 1942. informed me yesterday that Molotov had sent for him and had received him with great cordiality. He told him that so far as the Soviet Union is concerned, de GaulleGen. Charles de Gaulle, president of the French National Committee, leader of the Free French. is the true representative of the French people and that Russia desires the restoration of a strong France. He said that Molotov agreed in principle to a French division being sent to the Russian front provided the British were willing to release them. Garreau informed me that the matter has already been discussed with the British and that de Gaulle contemplates sending his best Syrian division.
